humanities_role_explanation: Includes tasks from creative and text-focused occupations like producers, directors, and editors as part of a wider economic evaluation of LLMs on real-world professional work.
benchmark_modalities_explanation: Evaluates models on complex multi-modal tasks where instructions and reference files are provided, and outputs can range from text documents and presentations to spreadsheets, diagrams, and multimedia.
analytical_tasks_explanation: Evaluates performance on complex real-world deliverables such as legal brief writing, video editing instructions, engineering conceptualization, and financial analysis.

general_purpose_benchmark.note: Evaluates model capabilities across 44 occupations spanning 9 major industries.

evaluates_generative_llm.note: Evaluates several leading generative models including GPT-4o, o4-mini, OpenAI o3, GPT-5, Claude Opus 4.1, Gemini 2.5 Pro, and Grok 4.

llm_is_primary_system.note: The primary focus is measuring generative LLM capabilities on economic tasks.

arts_humanities_data.note: Covers occupations such as Producers and Directors, Editors, and News Analysts/Reporters/Journalists, which fall under Visual Arts and Performing Arts, and Literature and Literary Theory.

quantitative_metrics.note: Measures win/tie/loss rates against human experts, cost, and speed of completion.

english_language.note: The paper and benchmark materials are in English.

summary: GDPval is a benchmark developed by OpenAI to measure LLM performance on 1,320 economically valuable, real-world knowledge work tasks across 44 occupations in 9 industries. The tasks are derived from real-world deliverables and graded blindly by experienced human professionals and an automated grader.
key_findings: Frontier models are starting to approach human expert quality on professional tasks; Claude Opus 4.1 performed best, producing outputs rated as good as or better than humans in nearly half of the tasks. Performance more than tripled from GPT-4o to GPT-5, and models completed tasks roughly 100x faster and 100x cheaper than human experts (excluding human oversight and iteration).
